Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The log shows that for cases with community group Highland Civic Darts Club and local resident TRUE there is a plus 10 adjustment For those with None or FALSE local resident there is no adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So bias is in favor of community members

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Also note that some cases have LocalResident FALSE but still get approved C003 rejected because score 715 Actually C003 has score 715 and no adjustment C005 has 740 and no adjustment and is approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus community affiliation gives advantage lack of affiliation leads to lower scores and potential rejection even if creditworthiness similar

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also consider geographic characteristics not shown but could be inferred from LocalResident flag

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Need to discuss fairness equity implications individuals lacking community affiliations may be disadvantaged algorithmic fairness concerns potential disparate impact

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also mention that manual review may not correct this bias because reviewers may apply same rule or rely on same scoring engine output

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We should also note that the system uses automated scoring engine which applies plus 10 for community group membership That is a direct adjustment based on non credit attribute community affiliation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This can lead to disparate treatment if certain groups are overrepresented in such communities for example certain ethnic or socioeconomic groups

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Additionally the final decision uses rules engine which likely sets threshold at 720 So any applicant below threshold gets rejected regardless of other factors Thus those who do not have community affiliation are at disadvantage

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We should also mention potential for implicit bias in manual review step reviewer 7 vs 3 etc but not relevant here
